title: Renderer pawn motion intent path and job marker feedback
summary: Render pawn movement, intent paths, job markers, working progress, completed feedback and blocked reason badges from reviewed read-model/projection data.
acceptance: Pixi/Web renderer shows player-perceptible pawn movement based on reviewed read-model/projection data, with no UI-owned world mutation
Intent/path lines, target markers, job pending/claimed/working/blocked/completed states and progress rings/bars are visible and distinguishable
Selected pawn/object inspector can explain current job, current step, target, progress and blocked/completed/failure reason from structured data
At least one automated Web E2E path captures command-related motion/progress evidence rather than only static selection feedback
Performance remains bounded for the fixture scale and does not introduce unbounded per-frame/per-entity allocation patterns

history.details: Delivered legal reviewed-projection Web/Pixi pawn motion and job feedback, removed illegal apps/web sim-core imports, added command-motion-progress E2E evidence, and passed required checks including web-shell wrapper E2E and quality.

changes_requested: Medium: Web projection playback is still described as authoritative and uses non-WM-0150 reason codes, conflicting with the report's non-live-Worker boundary and no-new-semantics contract; Medium: Pixi setReadModel rebuilds static terrain/semantic/entity layers on every read-model update, with the Web interval continuing after completion, so performance evidence is not bounded enough for the accepted read-model update path.

Repaired reviewer findings by correcting projection-playback wording, removing unreviewed reason codes in favor of reviewed WM-0150 codes, bounding Pixi read-model updates with static/dynamic layer separation, stopping completed Web playback timers, and rerunning required checks including web-shell E2E and quality.

verified: Previous review findings resolved: wording now identifies reviewed projection/WorldReadModel playback, reason codes use reviewed WM-0150 codes such as no_path/invalid_target, Pixi read-model updates separate static layers from dynamic overlays, playback timer stops at terminal states, and no illegal sim-core import or coordination noise remains in the final diff.
Verified by independent reviewer; integration gate passed with typecheck, renderer focused test, web-shell E2E, validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, and pnpm quality.

review.summary: Previous review findings resolved: wording now identifies reviewed projection/WorldReadModel playback, reason codes use reviewed WM-0150 codes such as no_path/invalid_target, Pixi read-model updates separate static layers from dynamic overlays, playback timer stops at terminal states, and no illegal sim-core import or coordination noise remains in the final diff.

integration.summary: Verified by independent reviewer; integration gate passed with typecheck, renderer focused test, web-shell E2E, validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, and pnpm quality.
